release_rule: Release only an immutable Git commit SHA or signed archive digest. The replicator must receive the same artifact set and the release identifier must be recorded before first execution.

required_artifact_groups.selection_rule: all frozen USD-W2 through USD-W5 scopes, candidate definitions, fixtures, obligation definitions, equivalence rules, cost ledgers, results, proofs, audits, and theorem-boundary artifacts

all registered negative controls plus an explicit R3 mutation corpus covering equivalence collapse, hidden-cost deletion, claim inflation, counterexample deletion, unique-minimum promotion, and independence-label inflation

public machine-readable schemas and source artifacts required to reconstruct the pipeline; original generated intermediates and executable implementation code are excluded unless independently justified as part of the frozen specification
canonical validators and tests used to check package structure, but clearly marked as reference checks rather than code the replicator may reuse for the independent implementation

replicator_delivery_views.specification_view: required; contains scopes, obligations, fixtures, schemas, costs, equivalence definitions, and claim boundaries
replicator_delivery_views.reference_result_view: sealed until the replicator freezes its implementation; contains terminal reference outputs and witness comparisons
replicator_delivery_views.original_code_view: not delivered for implementation reuse; may be released only after the initial result for forensic comparison

completeness_checks: the parent POST-USD-EVC-001 registration artifact is included
every mandatory target resolves to frozen source artifacts
every expected classification resolves to a registered result
the mutation corpus includes all seven mandatory mutations
all cost and equivalence definitions needed for independent reconstruction are included
reference outputs are separable from the initial specification view
original executable implementations are not silently included as dependencies
all unresolved gates and nonclaims are preserved
release state and first-access state remain false before delivery
nonclaims: the manifest itself proves package completeness
an independent replicator has received the package
the original implementation is independently validated
